text:
To conduct an experiment with the provided electrical circuit components, follow these procedures and take the necessary measurements:

**Step 1: Setting up the Circuit**
Connect the two dry cells in series to create a battery pack. This will be the power source for the circuit. Connect one end of the battery pack to one end of the resistor, and the other end of the resistor to one end of the rheostat. Connect the other end of the rheostat to one terminal of the switch/plug key. Connect the other terminal of the switch/plug key to one end of the ammeter, and the other end of the ammeter to the other end of the battery pack. Finally, connect the voltmeter in parallel across the resistor.

**Step 2: Measuring Initial Values**
Close the switch/plug key to complete the circuit. Record the initial reading on the ammeter, which represents the initial current (I) flowing through the circuit. Record the initial reading on the voltmeter, which represents the initial voltage (V) across the resistor.

**Step 3: Varying Resistance and Measuring**
Adjust the rheostat to different resistance values, taking care to record the resistance (R) at each setting. For each resistance setting, record the corresponding current (I) from the ammeter and voltage (V) from the voltmeter. Repeat this process for at least 5 different resistance settings.

**Step 4: Calculating Power**
Using the measured values of voltage (V) and current (I), calculate the power (P) dissipated by the resistor at each resistance setting using the formula: P = V × I. Record these power values.

**Step 5: Analyzing Results**
Plot a graph of current (I) against voltage (V) for the different resistance settings. This will help to verify Ohm's Law, which states that V = I × R. Also, plot a graph of power (P) against resistance (R) to observe the relationship between power dissipation and resistance.

**Measurements to be Taken:**

- Initial current (I) and voltage (V) with the switch/plug key closed
- Current (I) and voltage (V) at different resistance (R) settings
- Resistance (R) values at each setting
- Power (P) dissipated by the resistor at each resistance setting

**Calculations:**

- Power (P) = V × I
- Resistance (R) can be calculated using Ohm's Law: R = V / I

**Units:**

- Current (I): amperes (A)
- Voltage (V): volts (V)
- Resistance (R): ohms (Ω)
- Power (P): watts (W)
